originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: A Kind Helper from Tunisia!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter, here from the wonderful continent of Africa! Today, we have a very important story about a super kind person named Saadia Mosbah. She lives in a beautiful country in North Africa called Tunisia.

Who is Saadia Mosbah?

Saadia Mosbah is like a super friend to everyone. She loves helping people, especially those who come from other places. Imagine someone new coming to your school or your town. Saadia is the person who would welcome them with a big smile and help them feel at home. She is what we call an "activist." That means she works very hard to make sure everyone is treated fairly and kindly.

What is Racism?

Sometimes, people are treated unfairly just because they look different or come from another country. This is called racism. It’s like saying, "I don't want to play with you because your shirt is a different color than mine." It's not kind, and it makes people sad. Saadia believes that everyone, no matter where they are from or what they look like, deserves love and respect. She teaches people to be friends with everyone! Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! People from all these countries are unique and special!

Helping People Who Move (Migrants)

You know how birds sometimes fly to warmer places for the winter? Or how your family might move to a new town for a new job or a bigger house? Well, some people also move from one country to another. We call these people "migrants." They might be looking for a safer place, or a better school, or just a new adventure. Saadia Mosbah works hard to help these people. She wants to make sure they have a safe place to live and enough food to eat, and that they feel welcome.

A Big Worry in Tunisia

In Tunisia, the leader of the country is called President Kais Saied. Sometimes, the President worried that too many people were coming into Tunisia from other countries. He thought it might be a problem for the country. This made some people feel a bit scared or unsure about people arriving from different places. But Saadia had a different idea. She believed that everyone should be treated with kindness, no matter where they came from. She said, "Let's open our hearts and help these new friends!" She kept helping the migrants, showing everyone that kindness is always the best way. Did you know? Tunisia is a sunny country on the coast of North Africa, right next to the beautiful Mediterranean Sea!

Saadia's Brave Work

Saadia did not just talk about kindness; she showed it every day. She helped many people find food, clothes, and a safe place to sleep. She told stories about how wonderful it is when people from different places become friends and share their cultures. She wanted everyone in Tunisia to remember that we are all part of one big human family. Her voice was strong, and she taught many people how important it is to be fair to everyone.

What Happened to Saadia?

Because Saadia kept helping and speaking up for migrants, something difficult happened. The grown-ups who make the rules in Tunisia decided that Saadia had done something wrong by helping these people. She was told she had to go to a special place for a very long time, like a big timeout. It means she is not free to go home and help people right now. She is in a place called prison, for eight years. This is a very long time. It makes many people sad because Saadia was only trying to be kind and helpful. Did you know? Sometimes, people who stand up for what is right face big challenges. But their bravery can inspire many others!

Why This Story Matters

This story tells us something very important: it is always good to be kind. Saadia Mosbah showed us how to be brave and help people, even when it is hard. She reminded everyone that treating people unfairly because they are different is not right. We should always try to understand each other and share our smiles. Her kindness touched many hearts in Tunisia and around the world.

Be a Friend to Everyone!

Even though Saadia is not free right now, her message of love and kindness is still alive. We can all be like Saadia. We can make new friends, be kind to everyone, and remember that we are all special. When you see someone new, give them a friendly wave. When you play, include everyone. That’s how we make our world a better and happier place, just like Saadia taught us. Let's all be helpers and friends, just like her! Did you know? Helping others can make you feel happy too! It’s a wonderful feeling to share kindness.
summary: Saadia Mosbah, a kind helper from Tunisia, believed everyone should be treated fairly, even if they came from other places, but because she helped people, she is now in prison for eight years.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us to be kind to everyone, no matter where they come from, and to be brave in standing up for fairness, just like Saadia.

originalTitle: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
content: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.

originalTitle: Liverpool struggling because of lack of players - Slot
title: Liverpool struggling because of lack of players - Slot
content: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says injuries and lack of options in his squad is one of the reasons they are "struggling" following their 2-1 defeat by Brighton & Hove Albion, with injury to Hugo Ekitike adding to the absences of Mohamed Salah and Alexander Isak.

originalTitle: Swansea criticise TV coverage of Wrexham loss with rivals' owners as co-commentators
title: Swansea criticise TV coverage of Wrexham loss with rivals' owners as co-commentators
content: Swansea City chief executive Tom Gorringe criticises the television coverage of their Championship defeat at Wrexham, when the north Wales club's Hollywood owners were guest commentators.
